Name: Weedle
Effects: Search your deck for Weedle and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

Name: Kakuna
Flavor-Text: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: During your next turn, if the Defending Pokémon is damaged by an attack, it is Knocked Out.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

Name: Tangela
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
This attack does 10 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.

Name: Treecko
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Small hooks on the bottom of its feet catch on walls and ceilings. That is how it can hang from above.

Name: Grovyle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

Name: Sceptile
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [G] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, heal 30 damage from that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It agilely leaps about the jungle and uses the sharp leaves on its arms to strike its prey.

Name: Sceptile
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard 1 Energy attached to this Pokémon. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It agilely leaps about the jungle and uses the sharp leaves on its arms to strike its prey.

Name: Lotad
Flavor-Text: It searches about for clean water. If it does not drink water for too long, the leaf on its head wilts.

Name: Lombre
Flavor-Text: It has a mischievous spirit. If it spots an angler, it will tug on the fishing line to interfere.

Name: Ludicolo
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The rhythm of bright, festive music activates Ludicolo’s cells, making it more powerful.

Name: Surskit
Flavor-Text: It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.

Name: Masquerain
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flaps its four wings to hover and fly freely in any direction—to and fro and sideways.

Name: Shroomish
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

Name: Breloom
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage and your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

Name: Volbeat
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives around clean ponds. At night, its rear lights up. It converses with others by flashing its light.

Name: Illumise
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: With its sweet aroma, it guides Volbeat to draw signs with light in the night sky.

Name: Trevenant-EX
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

Name: Ninetales
Ability-Effect: Each player can’t play any Stadium cards from his or her hand.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1,000-year curse.

Name: Slugma
Effects: Discard a [G] Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn’t keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

Name: Magcargo
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

Name: Magcargo
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 60 more damage.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its body is as hot as lava and is always billowing. Flames will occasionally burst from its shell.

Name: Torchic
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Torchic
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may attack twice a turn. (If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Combusken
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: During a battle, the hot flame in its body increases. Its kicks have outstanding destructive power.

Name: Blaziken
Effects: Attach 3 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.

Name: Camerupt-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard as many [R] Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Horsea
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest in the shade of corals. If it senses danger, it spits murky ink and flees.

Name: Seadra
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is capable of swimming backwards by rapidly flapping its winglike pectoral fins and stout tail.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.

Name: Mudkip
Flavor-Text: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

Name: Marshtomp
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Living on muddy ground that provides poor footing has made its legs sturdy.

Name: Swampert
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

Name: Swampert
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a card. Shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.
Flavor-Text: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

Name: Ludicolo
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Splash Dance attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When this Pokémon is healed, double the amount healed.
Flavor-Text: The rhythm of bright, festive music activates Ludicolo’s cells, making it more powerful.

Name: Wailord-EX
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, remove all Special Conditions from it.
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Barboach
Flavor-Text: Its whiskers make a superb radar. They are used to locate prey, even in the murkiest of water.

Name: Whiscash
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

Name: Whiscash
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest at the bottom of swamps. It will eat anything—if it is alive, Whiscash will eat it.

Name: Corphish
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: Its hardy vitality enables it to adapt to any environment. Its pincers will never release prey.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms in schools and lives at the bottom of rivers.

Name: Milotic
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may put a card from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: You may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is the world’s most beautiful Pokémon. There are many works of art featuring Milotic.

Name: Spheal
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its body is covered in fluffy fur. The fur keeps it from feeling cold while it is rolling on ice.

Name: Spheal
Flavor-Text: They can’t swim well yet, and they move much faster by rolling. When they’re happy, they clap fins.

Name: Sealeo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Be it Spheal or a Poké Ball, it will spin any round object on its nose with the greatest of ease.

Name: Walrein
Effects: You may discard any Stadium card in play.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It shatters drift ice with its strong tusks. Its thick layer of blubber repels enemy attacks.

Name: Clamperl
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 50 or less.
Flavor-Text: It is protected by a sturdy shell. Once in a lifetime, it makes a magnificent pearl.

Name: Huntail
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep in the sea. With a tail shaped like a small fish, it attracts unsuspecting prey.

Name: Gorebyss
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its swimming form is exquisitely elegant. With its thin mouth, it feeds on seaweed that grows between rocks.

Name: Gorebyss
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.
Flavor-Text: Its swimming form is exquisitely elegant. With its thin mouth, it feeds on seaweed that grows between rocks.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.

Name: Kyogre-EX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Return 2 [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon to your hand.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Primal Kyogre-EX
Effects: Move 2 Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon-EX. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Primal-Reversion-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Kyogre-EX, your turn ends.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.

Name: Manaphy
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
Put 2 [W] Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Chinchou
Flavor-Text: It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.

Name: Lanturn
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It blinds prey with an intense burst of light. With the prey incapacitated, the Pokémon swallows it in a single gulp.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

Name: Electrike
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

Name: Manectric
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

Name: Tynamo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

Name: Eelektrik
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

Name: Eelektrik
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When this Pokémon is healed, double the amount healed.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

Name: Eelektross
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a basic Energy attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

Name: Nidoran Female
Flavor-Text: Small and very docile, it protects itself with its small, poisonous horn when attacked.

Name: Nidorina
Flavor-Text: When feeding its young, it first chews the food into a paste, then spits it out for the offspring.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Flavor-Text: The body is covered by stiff, needle-like scales. If it becomes excited, the needles bristle outwards.

Name: Nidoqueen
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself. Don’t apply Weakness to this damage.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may attack twice a turn. (If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The body is covered by stiff, needle-like scales. If it becomes excited, the needles bristle outwards.

Name: Tentacool
Effects: Return this Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.
Flavor-Text: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by their stingers.

Name: Tentacool
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When this Pokémon is healed, double the amount healed.
Flavor-Text: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by their stingers.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It has 80 tentacles that move about freely. They can sting, causing poisoning and sharp, stabbing pain.

Name: Starmie
Effects: If this Pokémon and your opponent’s Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is inept at turning because of its four short legs. It can only charge and run in one direction.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It begins walking on its hind legs after evolution. It can punch holes through boulders with its horn.

Name: Rhyperior
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. Geodude are shot at rare times.

Name: Rhyperior
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to your Pokémon by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. Geodude are shot at rare times.

Name: Nosepass
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its magnetic nose consistently faces north. Travelers check Nosepass to get their bearings.

Name: Meditite
Flavor-Text: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

Name: Medicham
Effects: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

Name: Medicham
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may attack twice a turn. (If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.

Name: Trapinch
Flavor-Text: It makes an inescapable conical pit and lies in wait at the bottom for prey to come tumbling down.

Name: Solrock
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Special Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs solar energy during the day. Always expressionless, it can sense what its foe is thinking.

Name: Groudon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon is said to represent the land. It went to sleep after dueling Kyogre.

Name: Groudon-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Primal Groudon-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Primal-Reversion-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Groudon-EX, your turn ends.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

Name: Hippopotas
Flavor-Text: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.
This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

Name: Drilbur
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: By spinning its body, it can dig straight through the ground at a speed of 30 mph.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: As powerful as an excavator, its ears can reduce dense bedrock to rubble. When it’s finished digging, it lounges lazily.

Name: Sharpedo-EX
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. Then, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Crawdaunt
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Loving to battle, this Pokémon pinches all Pokémon that enter its territory with its pincers and throws them out.

Name: Aggron-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Probopass
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels.

Name: Excadrill
Effects: Search your deck for 2 Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may attack twice a turn. (If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: More than 300 feet below the surface, they build mazelike nests. Their activity can be destructive to subway tunnels.

Name: Honedge
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person’s arm and drains that person’s life energy completely.

Name: Doublade
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon until its remaining HP is 10.
Flavor-Text: The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon has no Weakness.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

Name: Mr. Mime
Effects: Move a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of either player’s Pokémon to another of that player’s Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If there is no Pokémon to move the Pokémon Tool card to, this attack does nothing.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is adept at conning people. It is said to be able to create walls out of thin air by miming.

Name: Marill
Flavor-Text: The tip of its tail is filled with oil that is lighter than water, so it acts as a float.

Name: Azumarill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
You may do 30 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it’s in the water.

Name: Azumarill
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
This attack does 100 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When this Pokémon is healed, double the amount healed.
Flavor-Text: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it’s in the water.

Name: Gardevoir-EX
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Gardevoir-EX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of [Y] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kingdra
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life forms can survive.

Name: Kingdra
Effects: Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard a [W] and a [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon.

Name: Vibrava
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It generates ultrasonic waves by violently flapping its wings. After making its prey faint, it melts the prey with acid.

Name: Flygon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose either player. That player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck and draws 4 cards.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Desert Spirit,” this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

Name: Zigzagoon
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

Name: Linoone
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It charges prey at speeds over 60 mph. However, because it can only run straight, it often fails.

Name: Skitty
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It shows its cute side by chasing its own tail until it gets dizzy.

Name: Delcatty
Effects: Move as many Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is highly popular among female Trainers for its sublime fur. It does not keep a nest.

Name: Spinda
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, this Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The chances of two Spinda having identical spot patterns is less than one in four billion.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 more damage.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When this Pokémon is healed, double the amount healed.
Flavor-Text: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

Name: Bibarel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 80 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

Name: Bouffalant
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks do 40 more damage to your opponent’s [G] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has ears like shovels. Digging holes strengthens its ears so much that they can sever thick roots effortlessly.

Name: Bunnelby
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Shuffle a card from your discard pile into your deck.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: This Pokémon may attack twice a turn. (If the first attack Knocks Out your opponent’s Active Pokémon, you may attack again after your opponent chooses a new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has ears like shovels. Digging holes strengthens its ears so much that they can sever thick roots effortlessly.

Name: Acro Bike
Description: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Discard the other card.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Aggron Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Aggron-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Archie’s Ace in the Hole
Description: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.Put a [W] Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Dive Ball
Description: Search your deck for a [W] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Energy Retrieval
Description: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Escape Rope
Description: Each player switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. (Your opponent switches first. If a player does not have a Benched Pokémon, he or she doesn’t switch Pokémon.)
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Exp. Share
Description: When your Active Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, you may move 1 basic Energy card that was attached to that Pokémon to the Pokémon this card is attached to.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Fresh Water Set
Description: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Gardevoir Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gardevoir-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Groudon Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes Primal Groudon-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Kyogre Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes Primal Kyogre-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Maxie’s Hidden Ball Trick
Description: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.Put a [F] Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. Then, draw 5 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Professor Birch’s Observations
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck and flip a coin. If heads, draw 7 cards. If tails, draw 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Repeat Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon with the same name as 1 of your Pokémon in play, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Rough Seas
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may heal 30 damage from each of his or her [W] Pokémon and [L] Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Scorched Earth
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may discard a [R] or [F] Energy card from his or her hand. If that player does so, he or she draws 2 cards.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Shrine of Memories
Description: Each player’s evolved Pokémon can use any attack from its previous Evolutions. (That player still needs the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Silent Lab
Description: Each Basic Pokémon in play, in each player’s hand, and in each player’s discard pile has no Abilities.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Teammates
Description: You can play this card only if 1 of your Pokémon was Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Weakness Policy
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to has no Weakness.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Shield Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [M] Pokémon. This card provides [M] Energy only while this card is attached to a [M] Pokémon.The attacks of your opponent’s Pokémon do 10 less damage to the [M] Pokémon this card is attached to (before applying Weakness and Resistance).(If this card is attached to anything other than a [M] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Wonder Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to [Y] Pokémon. This card provides [Y] Energy only while this card is attached to a [Y] Pokémon.Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to the [Y] Pokémon that this card is attached to. (Existing effects are not removed.)(If this card is attached to anything other than a [Y] Pokémon, discard this card.)

Name: Trevenant-EX
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard as many [R] Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 50 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wailord-EX
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, remove all Special Conditions from it.
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Return 2 [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon to your hand.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Move 2 Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon-EX. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Primal-Reversion-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Kyogre-EX, your turn ends.
Ancient-Trait-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.

Name: Groudon-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Ancient-Trait-Effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

Name: Sharpedo-EX

ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
